prompts.text: What do you remember loving most about being a child?
prompts.followUp1: What about that still stays with you now?
prompts.followUp2: Do you think that part of childhood still lives somewhere in you?

prompts.text: What kind of play made you happiest when you were young?
prompts.followUp1: What did that kind of play let you feel?
prompts.followUp2: Do you think that part of you still shows up now?

prompts.text: What do you remember most clearly about the house you grew up in?
prompts.followUp1: What feeling comes back first when you picture it?
prompts.followUp2: What did that home give you, and what did it not give you?

prompts.text: Which family member felt safest to you when you were little?
prompts.followUp1: What made them feel safe?
prompts.followUp2: Do you think they knew what they were to you?

prompts.text: What did an ordinary afternoon feel like when you were young?
prompts.followUp1: What details come back most easily?
prompts.followUp2: What do you miss, if anything, about that pace of life?

prompts.text: What scared you when you were a child?
prompts.followUp1: Did anyone know how afraid you were?
prompts.followUp2: Do you think that fear shaped you in ways that still last?

prompts.text: What did you think adulthood was going to be like?
prompts.followUp1: What did you get right?
prompts.followUp2: What turned out to be completely different?

prompts.text: Who taught you to tie your shoes, and do you remember what that was like?
prompts.followUp1: What do you remember about the person who taught you?
prompts.followUp2: What small thing did they teach you that stayed with you longer than they probably knew?

prompts.text: What were you like in school?
prompts.followUp1: What did people tend to notice about you first?
prompts.followUp2: Do you think that was the real you, or just one version of you?

prompts.text: Which teacher do you still remember, and why?
prompts.followUp1: What did they see in you or awaken in you?
prompts.followUp2: Did they affect the way you saw yourself?

prompts.text: What kind of friends did you have growing up?
prompts.followUp1: What did those friendships give you then?
prompts.followUp2: What do you think they revealed about who you were becoming?

prompts.text: When you were young, what did you imagine you would become?
prompts.followUp1: What part of that dream stayed with you?
prompts.followUp2: What part had to change as life became real?

prompts.text: What did you love most about being young?
prompts.followUp1: What feeling from that time do you still miss?
prompts.followUp2: Have you found any version of it again later in life?

prompts.text: What did you find hardest about growing up?
prompts.followUp1: Did anyone really understand that at the time?
prompts.followUp2: What did it ask of you too early?

prompts.text: How was conflict between siblings or friends handled when you were growing up?
prompts.followUp1: What did that teach you about conflict at the time?
prompts.followUp2: Do you think you still handle conflict in some of those same ways now?

prompts.text: What was your first job?
prompts.followUp1: What did it teach you about people or responsibility?
prompts.followUp2: How did earning money for yourself first feel?

prompts.text: What job taught you the most about people?
prompts.followUp1: What did it show you that stayed with you?
prompts.followUp2: Did it change how you saw yourself too?

prompts.text: What responsibility came into your life earlier than you were ready for?
prompts.followUp1: How did you carry it?
prompts.followUp2: What do you think it changed in you?

prompts.text: What was a turning point in your early adult life?
prompts.followUp1: Did you know it was a turning point at the time?
prompts.followUp2: How did life divide before and after it?

prompts.text: What did work mean to you at different stages of life?
prompts.followUp1: Did it mean survival, pride, duty, identity, or something else?
prompts.followUp2: How did that meaning change over time?

prompts.text: Was there a job you loved more than people would expect?
prompts.followUp1: What made it feel meaningful to you?
prompts.followUp2: Do you think that job revealed something true about you?

prompts.text: If you served in the military, what do you wish people understood about that part of your life?
prompts.followUp1: What did that experience ask of you?
prompts.followUp2: How do you think it stayed with you afterward?

prompts.text: How did you meet your spouse or partner?
prompts.followUp1: What was your first real impression of them?
prompts.followUp2: When did you know this relationship mattered?

prompts.text: What was love like for you when you were young?
prompts.followUp1: How did that understanding change as you got older?
prompts.followUp2: What did love teach you that nothing else could?

prompts.text: What made your marriage or partnership easier?
prompts.followUp1: What kind of habits or qualities helped most?
prompts.followUp2: What do you think held you together in hard seasons?

prompts.text: What made it harder?
prompts.followUp1: What do you understand now that you didn't understand then?
prompts.followUp2: What do you wish younger people knew about lasting love?

prompts.text: What did you first admire about the person you chose to build a life with?
prompts.followUp1: Did that admiration change over time, or deepen?
prompts.followUp2: What do you think they brought out in you?

prompts.text: What surprised you most about becoming a parent?
prompts.followUp1: What part of it changed you most?
prompts.followUp2: What did you not understand until you were living it?

prompts.text: What part of parenting brought you the most joy?
prompts.followUp1: What kind of moments stay with you most now?
prompts.followUp2: What did those moments reveal about you?

prompts.text: What part of parenting felt loneliest or hardest?
prompts.followUp1: Did you feel understood in that difficulty?
prompts.followUp2: What did you need more of at the time?

prompts.text: Is there anything you wish you had done differently as a parent?
prompts.followUp1: What do you understand about that now?
prompts.followUp2: What do you hope your children understand about you, even there?

prompts.text: What do you think your children understood well about you, and what do you think they missed?
prompts.followUp1: What do you wish they had seen more clearly?
prompts.followUp2: Do you think they understand it better now?

prompts.text: What was one of the hardest seasons of your life?
prompts.followUp1: What got you through it when you didn't know how you would?
prompts.followUp2: What did that season ask of you?

prompts.text: Was there a loss that changed you permanently?
prompts.followUp1: How did it shape the way you live or love now?
prompts.followUp2: What does that loss still ask of you today?

prompts.text: What sacrifice did you make that people didn't fully see?
prompts.followUp1: Did you feel it was recognized enough?
prompts.followUp2: Would you make it again?

prompts.text: What made you keep going when things felt very heavy?
prompts.followUp1: Was it duty, love, faith, stubbornness, hope, or something else?
prompts.followUp2: Do you think that strength changed you?

prompts.text: What value has guided your life the most?
prompts.followUp1: Where do you think that value came from?
prompts.followUp2: How did it shape the choices you made?

prompts.text: What belief did you have to let go of over time?
prompts.followUp1: What changed it?
prompts.followUp2: What did letting it go make possible?

prompts.text: What do you think makes someone a good person?
prompts.followUp1: Has your answer changed with age?
prompts.followUp2: What taught you that most deeply?

prompts.text: What do you believe now that your younger self might not have understood?
prompts.followUp1: What would your younger self have resisted hearing?
prompts.followUp2: Why does it feel true to you now?

prompts.text: What did your own parents or grandparents teach you that stayed with you the longest?
prompts.followUp1: Was it something they said, or something they modeled?
prompts.followUp2: Do you think you passed it on too?

prompts.text: Is there something you wish you had asked your own parents before they were gone?
prompts.followUp1: What do you think you were really wanting to know?
prompts.followUp2: Do you still feel that absence now?

prompts.text: What do you wish you had known earlier in life?
prompts.followUp1: Would knowing it earlier really have changed things?
prompts.followUp2: How did you finally learn it?

prompts.text: What do you wish you had been brave enough to do?
prompts.followUp1: What stopped you at the time?
prompts.followUp2: How do you feel about it now?

prompts.text: What decision are you proud you made?
prompts.followUp1: What did that decision protect or create?
prompts.followUp2: How did it shape the life that followed?

prompts.text: What question do you wish someone had asked you sooner in life?
prompts.followUp1: Why do you think nobody asked it?
prompts.followUp2: What becomes possible when someone finally does?

prompts.text: What do you hope people remember about how you loved?
prompts.followUp1: What matters most to you in that memory?
prompts.followUp2: Do you think the people closest to you know that now?

prompts.text: What do you hope outlives you?
prompts.followUp1: Is it something you built, taught, gave, or embodied?
prompts.followUp2: Where do you hope it keeps living?

prompts.text: What lesson do you hope your family carries forward?
prompts.followUp1: What taught you that lesson?
prompts.followUp2: Why does it matter so much to you?

prompts.text: If someone in your family listened closely to your life, what would you most want them to understand?
prompts.followUp1: What do you hope they never miss about who you were?
prompts.followUp2: What would it mean to feel truly understood in that way?
